svg.title: Simulated REALM SHAP-style attribution on EEG
svg.desc: Eight synthetic EEG channels are plotted from zero to two seconds. A seizure begins at 0.68 seconds and ends at 1.36 seconds. Warm orange positive attribution is concentrated over the rhythmic ictal activity and is strongest in central and parietal channels. These are simulated EEG and attribution values, not patient data or measured model output.

cc:Work.dc:description: Eight synthetic EEG channels are plotted from zero to two seconds. A seizure begins at 0.68 seconds and ends at 1.36 seconds. Warm orange positive attribution is concentrated over the rhythmic ictal activity and is strongest in central and parietal channels. These are simulated EEG and attribution values, not patient data or measured model output.

g.text: REALM Stage 4: positive attribution on an eight-channel EEG trace

text.style: font-size: 10.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start; fill: #687381

g.text: Warm shading encodes simulated seizure-supporting attribution; traces retain amplitude in microvolts.

path.style: fill: #ffffff; stroke: #d97706; stroke-width: 1.1; stroke-linejoin: miter

font-size: 8.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: middle; fill: #687381

g.text: SIMULATED source rows include time, channel, signal amplitude, attribution, seizure state, sampling rate, and fixed random seed.
